page: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs (2 physical cores, 1.0 thread per core) running at 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits a mix of top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test ranks in the top 10% with a score of 3434.78, while the multi-core CPU test falls into the middle 50% at 6819.14. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, with gzip compression reaching 32.16 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.65 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak, scoring 326.91 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write bandwidths remain average. Passmark CPU and database benchmarks are positioned in the bottom 25%, and static web serving throughput is poor at 2.49 GB/sec.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is a notable tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, predictable compute power. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-focused tasks that do not require dedicated hardware, leveraging the advanced instruction sets of the AMD Genoa architecture, such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration (AES, SHA_NI). It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic batch processing, development environments, and small-scale text generation tasks using compact models.
description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an entry-level instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While its stress-ng single-core performance ranks in the top tier, its multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into average or weak categories due to its shared CPU allocation. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES cryptography. This instance offers a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded execution but do not require dedicated CPU resources. It is best suited for lightweight batch processing, development environments, and small-scale single-threaded applications.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage for budget-friendly compute workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-core instance pairing strong single-threaded AMD Genoa performance with fast NVMe storage for lightweight compute workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM
Bundled 50 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and development environments
